---
title: "plot_code_CCZV"
author: "Bowen"
date: "2026-06-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Fig. 2

## Fig. 2a
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Relative proportion of annotated vs unannotated taxa
# across taxonomic levels
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(ggplot2)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
df_2a <- readRDS("df_2a.rds")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Set factor order
# -----------------------------
df_2a$Taxonomy <- factor(
  df_2a$Taxonomy,
  levels = c("Realm", "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family")
)

df_2a$type <- factor(
  df_2a$type,
  levels = c("Unannotated", "Annotated")
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Define fill colors
# -----------------------------
fill_colors <- c("#cccccc", "#c3d7f2")

# -----------------------------
# 5. Plot
# -----------------------------
p_2a <- ggplot(
  df_2a,
  aes(x = Taxonomy, y = 100 * Proportion, fill = type)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Taxonomy level",
    y = "Relative proportion (%)",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15, angle = 90),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1, fill = NA),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 1)
  )

p_2a
```

## Fig. 2b
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Donut chart of temperate vs unknown viruses
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Input data
# -----------------------------
donut_df <- data.frame(
  group = c("Temperate viruses", "Unknown"),
  value = c(1060, 10682)
)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Calculate percentages and label positions
# -----------------------------
donut_df <- donut_df %>%
  mutate(
    pct = value / sum(value) * 100,
    label = paste0(round(pct, 1), "%"),
    ymax = cumsum(value),
    ymin = lag(ymax, default = 0),
    ypos = (ymax + ymin) / 2
  )

# -----------------------------
# 4. Define fill colors
# -----------------------------
fill_colors <- c(
  "Temperate viruses" = "#5679BA",
  "Unknown" = "#E6E6E6"
)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Plot
# -----------------------------
p_2b <- ggplot(donut_df, aes(x = 2, y = value, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(x = 2, y = ypos, label = label),
    size = 7,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0.5, 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

p_2b

```

## Fig. 2c
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Beta-diversity analysis of vOTU communities
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(zCompositions)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
# Required objects in df_2c.rds:
# - vOTU_count: abundance table (first column = vOTU ID, remaining columns = samples)
# - meta: sample metadata table containing at least Sample and Group
dat_list <- readRDS("df_2c.rds")

vOTU_count <- dat_list$vOTU_count
meta <- dat_list$meta

# -----------------------------
# 3. Format abundance matrix
# -----------------------------
otu_df <- as.data.frame(vOTU_count)

votu_ids <- as.character(otu_df[[1]])
rownames(otu_df) <- votu_ids
otu_df[[1]] <- NULL

otu_mat <- as.matrix(otu_df)
otu_mat <- apply(otu_mat, 2, as.numeric)
rownames(otu_mat) <- votu_ids
otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Match samples between abundance matrix and metadata
# -----------------------------
sample_set <- as.character(meta$Sample)
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(otu_mat), sample_set)

stopifnot(length(common_samples) >= 2)

otu_mat <- otu_mat[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]

meta <- meta %>%
  filter(Sample %in% common_samples) %>%
  arrange(match(Sample, common_samples))

# -----------------------------
# 5. Optional: calculate occurrence rate
# -----------------------------
occurrence_rate <- colSums(otu_mat != 0, na.rm = TRUE) / nrow(otu_mat)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Zero replacement
#    zCompositions::cmultRepl with CZM method
# -----------------------------
otu_z <- cmultRepl(
  X = otu_mat,
  label = 0,
  method = "CZM",
  output = "p-counts"
)

# -----------------------------
# 7. CLR transformation
# -----------------------------
otu_clr <- decostand(otu_z, method = "clr")

# -----------------------------
# 8. Aitchison distance + PERMANOVA
# -----------------------------
dist_aitchison <- dist(otu_clr, method = "euclidean")

set.seed(123)
perm_div <- adonis2(
  dist_aitchison ~ Group,
  data = meta,
  permutations = 999
)

print(perm_div)

adonis_label <- paste0(
  "adonis R2: ", round(perm_div$R2[1], 2),
  "; P-value: ", perm_div$`Pr(>F)`[1]
)

adonis_label

# -----------------------------
# 9. PCA
# -----------------------------
pca <- prcomp(otu_clr, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)

var_exp <- (pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2)

pc_df <- as.data.frame(pca$x[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Sample") %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "Sample")

# -----------------------------
# 10. Plot PCA
# -----------------------------
p_2c <- ggplot(pc_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, fill = Group)) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Group), size = 5) +
  stat_ellipse(
    aes(color = Group),
    type = "norm",
    linetype = 2,
    linewidth = 0.7,
    geom = "path",
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", sprintf("%.2f", 100 * var_exp[1]), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", sprintf("%.2f", 100 * var_exp[2]), "%)")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#5679ba", "#ee553d", "#c6e9f4", "#ffe094")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#5679ba", "#ee553d", "#c6e9f4", "#ffe094")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 21:24) +
  annotate(
    geom = "text",
    x = 0,
    y = 0.35,
    label = adonis_label,
    size = 8
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(colour = "black", size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(colour = "black", size = 20),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black", size = 15),
    legend.background = element_rect(color = "#808080", linetype = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 15, color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 25),
    strip.background = element_rect(color = "black"),
    panel.border = element_rect(linewidth = 1)
  )

p_2c

```

## Fig. 2d
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Microdiversity comparison across sediment depth layers
# =========================================================

library(tidyverse)   # dplyr, ggplot2, readr, tidyr
library(rstatix)     # compare_means, kruskal_test, etc.
library(ggpubr)      # stat_compare_means
library(openxlsx)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Input data
# -----------------------------

df_2d <- readRDS("df_2d.rds")

# Set depth order for plotting and statistics
df_2d$depth <- factor(
  df_2d$depth,
  levels = c("0-2", "4-6", "8-10", "14-16")
)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Number of viruses per sample
# -----------------------------
sample_counts <- df_2d %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(n_virus = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(n_virus)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Custom function:
#    randomly subsample 50 pi values per sample,
#    repeat 1,000 times, and return the average
# -----------------------------
subsample_pi <- function(df, n = 50, times = 1000) {
  means <- replicate(times, {
    sampled <- sample(df$pi, size = min(n, nrow(df)), replace = FALSE)
    mean(sampled, na.rm = TRUE)
  })
  mean(means, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Calculate microdiversity for each sample
# -----------------------------
result <- df_2d %>%
  group_by(source, depth) %>%
  summarise(
    microdiversity = subsample_pi(cur_data_all()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(result)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Plot settings
# -----------------------------
# Colors for depth groups
my_colors <- c("#5679ba", "#c6e9f4", "#ffe094", "#ee553d")

# Point shape mapping (kept for consistency)
shape_mapping <- c(
  "0-2"   = 21,
  "4-6"   = 21,
  "8-10"  = 21,
  "14-16" = 21
)

# Pairwise comparisons for significance testing
my_comparisons <- list(
  c("0-2", "4-6"),
  c("0-2", "8-10"),
  c("0-2", "14-16"),
  c("4-6", "8-10"),
  c("4-6", "14-16"),
  c("8-10", "14-16")
)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Pairwise Wilcoxon tests
# -----------------------------
pwc <- compare_means(
  microdiversity ~ depth,
  data = result,
  comparisons = my_comparisons,
  method = "wilcox.test"
) %>%
  mutate(
    p.adj = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"),
    p.adj.signif = symnum(
      p.adj,
      cutpoints = c(0, 1e-4, 1e-3, 1e-2, 0.05, 1),
      symbols = c("****", "***", "**", "*", "ns")
    )
  )

# Add y positions for significance labels
ymax <- max(result$microdiversity, na.rm = TRUE)
ymin <- min(result$microdiversity, na.rm = TRUE)
step <- 0.06 * (ymax - ymin)

pwc <- pwc %>%
  arrange(group1, group2) %>%
  mutate(y.position = ymax + step * seq_len(n()))

# -----------------------------
# 7. Kruskal-Wallis test
# -----------------------------
kw <- kruskal.test(microdiversity ~ depth, data = result)

kw_df <- data.frame(
  test = "Kruskal-Wallis",
  statistic = unname(kw$statistic),
  df = unname(kw$parameter),
  p.value = kw$p.value,
  method = kw$method,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Safety check
stopifnot("y.position" %in% names(pwc))

# -----------------------------
# 8. Plot
# -----------------------------
p_2d <- ggplot(
  result,
  aes(x = depth, y = microdiversity, fill = depth)
) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = depth),
    width = 0.20,
    size = 1,
    alpha = 0.6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 16) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid      = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 16),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pwc,
    label = "p.adj.signif",
    y.position = "y.position",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "microdiversity (pi)")

p_2d


```

## Fig. 2e
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Compare pNpS ratio across sediment depth layers 
# =========================================================

library(tidyverse)   # dplyr, ggplot2, readr, tidyr
library(rstatix)     # compare_means and related stats helpers
library(ggpubr)      # stat_pvalue_manual
library(openxlsx)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Input data
# -----------------------------
df_2e <- readRDS("df_2e.rds")

# Set depth order for plotting and statistical tests
df_2e$depth <- factor(
  df_2e$depth,
  levels = c("0-2", "4-6", "8-10", "14-16")
)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Pairwise group comparisons
# -----------------------------
my_comparisons <- list(
  c("0-2", "4-6"),
  c("0-2", "8-10"),
  c("0-2", "14-16"),
  c("4-6", "8-10"),
  c("4-6", "14-16"),
  c("8-10", "14-16")
)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Global Kruskal-Wallis test
# -----------------------------
kw <- kruskal.test(pNpS_ratio ~ depth, data = df_2e)

kw_df <- data.frame(
  test = "Kruskal-Wallis",
  statistic = unname(kw$statistic),
  df = unname(kw$parameter),
  p.value = kw$p.value,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# For consistency and future reuse, BH-FDR is also recorded here
kw_df$p.adj.BH <- p.adjust(kw_df$p.value, method = "BH")

kw_label <- paste0(
  "KW p = ", signif(kw_df$p.value, 3),
  " (BH-FDR = ", signif(kw_df$p.adj.BH, 3), ")"
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Pairwise Wilcoxon tests + BH-FDR
# -----------------------------
pwc <- compare_means(
  pNpS_ratio ~ depth,
  data = df_2e,
  comparisons = my_comparisons,
  method = "wilcox.test"
) %>%
  mutate(
    p.adj = p.adjust(p, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    p.adj.signif = case_when(
      p.adj <= 1e-4 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 1e-3 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 1e-2 ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 5. Y positions for significance labels
#    This prevents overlap among pairwise comparisons
# -----------------------------
ymax <- max(df_2e$pNpS_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
ymin <- min(df_2e$pNpS_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)
step <- 0.08 * (ymax - ymin)

# Fallback in case all values are identical
if (!is.finite(step) || step == 0) step <- 0.1

pwc <- pwc %>%
  arrange(group1, group2) %>%
  mutate(y.position = ymax + step * seq_len(n()))

# -----------------------------
# 6. Plot settings
# -----------------------------
my_colors <- c("#5679ba", "#c6e9f4", "#ffe094", "#ee553d")

# Note:
# shape_mapping is retained for consistency with similar scripts,
# although shape is not explicitly mapped in this plot.
shape_mapping <- c(
  "0-2"   = 21,
  "4-6"   = 21,
  "8-10"  = 21,
  "14-16" = 21
)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Plot
# -----------------------------
p_2e <- ggplot(
  df_2e,
  aes(x = depth, y = pNpS_ratio, fill = depth)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.6,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    size = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = depth),
    width = 0.20,
    size = 1,
    alpha = 0.6,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = 16) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "text",
    aes(label = round(after_stat(y), 2)),
    vjust = -0.5,
    color = "black",
    size = 4
  ) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    pwc,
    label = "p.adj.signif",
    y.position = "y.position",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    tip.length = 0.01,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "pNpS ratio")

p_2e

```

## Fig. 2f
```{r cars}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Input data
# -----------------------------
df_2f <- readRDS("df_2f.rds")

# -----------------------------
# 2. Set factor order
# -----------------------------
df_2f$group <- factor(
  df_2f$group,
  levels = c("0-2", "4-6", "8-10", "14-16")
)

df_2f$Function <- factor(
  df_2f$Function,
  levels = c(
    "DNA replication and repair & Metabolism",
    "Transcription and translation",
    "Assembly and packaging",
    "Lysis and lysogeny",
    "Structural proteins",
    "Others"
  )
)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Color palette
# -----------------------------
fill_colors <- c(
  "#5679ba", "#c6e9f4", "#ffe094",
  "#ffa45c", "#ee553d", "#CCCCCC"
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Plot
# -----------------------------
p_2f <- ggplot(
  df_2f,
  aes(x = group, y = 100 * value, fill = Function)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  labs(
    x = "Depth",
    y = "Relative proportion (%)",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15, angle = 90),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 1, fill = NA),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.length = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 1)
  )

p_2f

```

# Fig. 3

## Fig. 3b
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Five-set Venn diagram
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(VennDiagram)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
df_3b <- readRDS("df_3b.rds")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Helper function:
#    remove empty strings and NA values
# -----------------------------
clean_values <- function(x) {
  na.omit(x[x != ""])
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Extract and clean each set
# -----------------------------
venn_sets <- list(
  Refseq   = clean_values(df_3b$RefSeq),
  Seawater = clean_values(df_3b$Seawater),
  Coldseep = clean_values(df_3b$Coldseep),
  Trench   = clean_values(df_3b$Trench),
  CCZ      = clean_values(df_3b$CCZ)
)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Define colors
# -----------------------------
venn_colors <- c(
  "dodgerblue",
  "goldenrod1",
  "darkorange1",
  "seagreen3",
  "orchid3"
)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Draw and export Venn diagram
# -----------------------------
venn.diagram(
  x = venn_sets,
  scaled = FALSE,
  alpha = 0.8,
  lwd = 1,
  lty = 1,
  col = venn_colors,
  label.col = "black",
  cex = 1,
  fontface = "bold",
  fill = venn_colors,
  category.names = names(venn_sets),
  cat.dist = 0.03,
  cat.cex = 1,
  cat.fontface = "bold",
  cat.col = "black",
  cat.default.pos = "text",
  output = TRUE,
  filename = "./vnn.svg",
  imagetype = "svg",
  resolution = 400,
  compression = "lzw",
  width = 5,
  height = 5
)

```

## Fig. 3c
```{r cars}
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(ComplexUpset)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(readr)

# =========================================================
# Integrated analysis of shared clusters across deep-sea habitats
# Includes:
# 1) UpSet plot of CCZV-shared clusters
# 2) Horizontal bar plot of total vs shared clusters
# 3) Donut plots of functional composition for shared groups
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load input data
# -----------------------------
input_data <- readRDS("df_3c.rds")
dat <- input_data$pc_deepsea
# -----------------------------
# 2. Global settings
# -----------------------------
sets <- c("CCZV", "HV", "coldseep", "OTVGD")

habitat_colors <- c(
  CCZV     = "#D55E00",
  HV       = "#009E73",
  coldseep = "#CC79A7",
  OTVGD    = "#E69F00"
)

func_levels <- c(
  "anti-host defense",
  "exit",
  "gene expression",
  "integration",
  "other",
  "packaging",
  "replication",
  "structural",
  "unknown",
  "no_annotation"
)

func_palette <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(12, "Set3")
func_colors <- setNames(func_palette[1:length(func_levels)], func_levels)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Detect required columns
# -----------------------------
id_col <- dplyr::coalesce(
  if ("protein" %in% names(dat)) "protein" else NA_character_,
  if ("contig"  %in% names(dat)) "contig"  else NA_character_
)

if (is.na(id_col)) stop("Missing protein/contig column.", call. = FALSE)
if (!"cluster" %in% names(dat)) stop("Missing cluster column.", call. = FALSE)
if (!"function" %in% names(dat)) stop("Missing function column.", call. = FALSE)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Prepare protein-level table
# -----------------------------
dat1 <- dat %>%
  transmute(
    cluster = .data[["cluster"]],
    protein = .data[[id_col]],
    func = as.character(.data[["function"]])
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    func = ifelse(is.na(func) | func == "" | func == "NA", "no_annotation", func),
    func = factor(func, levels = func_levels),
    habitat = case_when(
      str_detect(protein, "^CCZV|^FN") ~ "CCZV",
      str_detect(protein, "^HV") ~ "HV",
      str_detect(protein, "^coldseep") ~ "coldseep",
      str_detect(protein, "^OTVGD") ~ "OTVGD",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(habitat)) %>%
  mutate(habitat = factor(habitat, levels = sets))

# -----------------------------
# 5. Build cluster-by-habitat matrix
# -----------------------------
cl_hab <- dat1 %>%
  distinct(cluster, habitat)

mat_all <- cl_hab %>%
  mutate(val = 1L) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = habitat, values_from = val, values_fill = 0L)

for (m in setdiff(sets, names(mat_all))) mat_all[[m]] <- 0L

mat_all <- mat_all %>%
  select(cluster, all_of(sets))

# Keep only clusters shared between CCZV and at least one other habitat
mat_cczv_shared <- mat_all %>%
  filter(CCZV == 1L) %>%
  filter(HV == 1L | coldseep == 1L | OTVGD == 1L)

if (nrow(mat_cczv_shared) == 0) {
  stop("No clusters shared between CCZV and other habitats were detected.", call. = FALSE)
}

# -----------------------------
# 6. UpSet plot
# -----------------------------
p_upset <- ComplexUpset::upset(
  mat_cczv_shared,
  rev(sets),
  sort_sets = FALSE,
  name = "Habitat",
  min_size = 1,
  width_ratio = 0.15,
  set_sizes = upset_set_size() + ylab("Number of shared clusters"),
  matrix = (
    intersection_matrix(geom = geom_point(shape = "circle filled", size = 5)) +
      scale_color_manual(
        values = habitat_colors,
        guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = "circle filled"))
      )
  ),
  queries = list(
    upset_query(set = "CCZV",     fill = habitat_colors["CCZV"]),
    upset_query(set = "HV",       fill = habitat_colors["HV"]),
    upset_query(set = "coldseep", fill = habitat_colors["coldseep"]),
    upset_query(set = "OTVGD",    fill = habitat_colors["OTVGD"])
  )
)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Horizontal bar plot:
#    total clusters vs clusters shared with CCZV
# -----------------------------
sum_bar_df <- tibble(habitat = sets) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(mat_all[[habitat]] == 1L),
    shared_with_cczv = if (habitat == "CCZV") {
      sum(mat_all$CCZV == 1L & (mat_all$HV == 1L | mat_all$coldseep == 1L | mat_all$OTVGD == 1L))
    } else {
      sum(mat_all[[habitat]] == 1L & mat_all$CCZV == 1L)
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    habitat = factor(habitat, levels = rev(sets)),
    total_plot = log10(total),
    shared_plot = log10(shared_with_cczv)
  )

p_bar <- ggplot(sum_bar_df, aes(y = habitat)) +
  geom_col(aes(x = total_plot), fill = "grey85", width = 0.70) +
  geom_col(aes(x = shared_plot, fill = habitat), width = 0.70) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = habitat_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_reverse(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15)),
    breaks = pretty_breaks(n = 5),
    labels = function(x) label_number(big.mark = ",")(10^x)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Number of clusters (log10; reversed)", y = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())

# -----------------------------
# 8. Convert membership pattern to shared-group labels
# -----------------------------
pattern_to_label <- function(pat, sets) {
  bits <- strsplit(pat, "")[[1]]
  on_sets <- sets[which(bits == "1")]
  paste(on_sets, collapse = "+")
}

cl_group <- mat_cczv_shared %>%
  mutate(pattern = apply(across(all_of(sets)), 1, paste0, collapse = "")) %>%
  transmute(
    cluster,
    group = vapply(pattern, pattern_to_label, character(1), sets = sets)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 9. Summarize protein-level functional composition
# -----------------------------
prot_group <- dat1 %>%
  inner_join(cl_group, by = "cluster")

sum_func_df <- prot_group %>%
  count(group, func, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    func = factor(func, levels = func_levels),
    group = factor(group, levels = sort(unique(group)))
  )

readr::write_tsv(sum_func_df, "intersection_func_summary.tsv")

# -----------------------------
# 10. Donut plot function
# -----------------------------
make_donut <- function(df_one, title_text, show_legend = FALSE) {
  df_one <- df_one %>% filter(n > 0)

  ggplot(df_one, aes(x = 2, y = pct, fill = func)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.85, color = "white", linewidth = 0.25) +
    coord_polar(theta = "y") +
    xlim(0.7, 2.6) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = func_colors, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_void() +
    labs(title = title_text, fill = "Function") +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
      legend.position = if (show_legend) "right" else "none",
      legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
    ) +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = 0,
      y = 0,
      label = paste0("n=", format(sum(df_one$n), big.mark = ",")),
      size = 3.1
    )
}

# -----------------------------
# 11. Build all donut plots
# -----------------------------
groups <- levels(sum_func_df$group)

plots <- lapply(seq_along(groups), function(i) {
  g <- groups[i]
  make_donut(
    sum_func_df %>% filter(group == g),
    title_text = g,
    show_legend = (i == 1)
  )
})

p_all <- wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 3)

# -----------------------------
# 12. Print plots
# -----------------------------
p_upset
p_bar
p_all


```

# Fig. 4

## Fig. 4b
```{r cars}

# =========================================================
# Sankey diagram of host taxonomy and viral family links
# =========================================================

library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(networkD3)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(webshot2)
library(openxlsx)
library(writexl)
library(cols4all)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Input data
# -----------------------------
df_4b <- readRDS("df_4b.rds")

# -----------------------------
# 2. Color palette
# -----------------------------
# Use a 20-color palette from cols4all and randomize color order
base_colors <- c4a("classic20", 20)
link_colors <- sample(base_colors, length(base_colors))

# -----------------------------
# 3. Standardize column names and clean taxonomic prefixes
# -----------------------------
df_4b <- df_4b %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Host_Genus  = Genus,
    Host_Phylum = Phyla,
    Count       = number,
    Host_Domain = Domain
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Host_Genus  = str_remove(Host_Genus, "^g__"),
    Host_Phylum = str_remove(Host_Phylum, "^p__"),
    Host_Domain = str_remove(Host_Domain, "^d__"),
    Host_Genus  = str_trim(Host_Genus),
    Host_Phylum = str_trim(Host_Phylum),
    Host_Domain = str_trim(Host_Domain),
    Count       = as.numeric(Count)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Host_Domain),
    !is.na(Host_Phylum),
    !is.na(Host_Genus),
    !is.na(Virus)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 4. Merge duplicated paths
# -----------------------------
df_4b <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Domain, Host_Phylum, Host_Genus, Virus) %>%
  summarise(Count = sum(Count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# -----------------------------
# 5. Parameters for low-abundance merging
# -----------------------------
TOPN_PHYLUM <- 10
TOPN_GENUS  <- 12
TOPN_VIRUS  <- 12
TRUNC_WIDTH <- 26

# -----------------------------
# 6. Compute total abundance at each level
# -----------------------------
phylum_tot <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Phylum) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(Count), .groups = "drop")

genus_tot <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Genus) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(Count), .groups = "drop")

virus_tot <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Virus) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(Count), .groups = "drop")

top_phylum <- phylum_tot %>%
  slice_max(n, n = TOPN_PHYLUM, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(Host_Phylum)

top_genus <- genus_tot %>%
  slice_max(n, n = TOPN_GENUS, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(Host_Genus)

top_virus <- virus_tot %>%
  slice_max(n, n = TOPN_VIRUS, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(Virus)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Collapse low-abundance groups into "Other"
# -----------------------------
df_4b <- df_4b %>%
  mutate(
    Host_Phylum = ifelse(Host_Phylum %in% top_phylum, Host_Phylum, "Other phyla"),
    Host_Genus  = ifelse(Host_Genus  %in% top_genus,  Host_Genus,  "Other genera"),
    Virus       = ifelse(Virus       %in% top_virus,  Virus,       "Other families")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Host_Domain, Host_Phylum, Host_Genus, Virus) %>%
  summarise(Count = sum(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Host_Phylum = str_trunc(Host_Phylum, TRUNC_WIDTH),
    Host_Genus  = str_trunc(Host_Genus, TRUNC_WIDTH),
    Virus       = str_trunc(Virus, TRUNC_WIDTH)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 8. Set factor order
# -----------------------------
df_4b <- df_4b %>%
  mutate(
    Host_Domain = factor(Host_Domain, levels = c("Bacteria", "Archaea")),
    Host_Phylum = fct_infreq(Host_Phylum),
    Host_Genus  = fct_infreq(Host_Genus),
    Virus       = fct_infreq(Virus)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 9. Build node table
# -----------------------------
nodes <- bind_rows(
  tibble(name = levels(df_4b$Host_Domain), axis = "Host Domain"),
  tibble(name = levels(df_4b$Host_Phylum), axis = "Host Phylum"),
  tibble(name = levels(df_4b$Host_Genus),  axis = "Host Genus"),
  tibble(name = levels(df_4b$Virus),       axis = "Virus Family")
) %>%
  mutate(id = row_number() - 1)

# -----------------------------
# 10. Build link table
#     Domain -> Phylum -> Genus -> Virus
#     Links are colored by phylum
# -----------------------------
links1 <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Domain, Host_Phylum) %>%
  summarise(value = sum(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Host Domain") %>% select(name, source = id),
    by = c("Host_Domain" = "name")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Host Phylum") %>% select(name, target = id),
    by = c("Host_Phylum" = "name")
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = as.character(Host_Phylum)) %>%
  select(source, target, value, group)

links2 <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Phylum, Host_Genus) %>%
  summarise(value = sum(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Host Phylum") %>% select(name, source = id),
    by = c("Host_Phylum" = "name")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Host Genus") %>% select(name, target = id),
    by = c("Host_Genus" = "name")
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = as.character(Host_Phylum)) %>%
  select(source, target, value, group)

links3 <- df_4b %>%
  group_by(Host_Phylum, Host_Genus, Virus) %>%
  summarise(value = sum(Count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Host Genus") %>% select(name, source = id),
    by = c("Host_Genus" = "name")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    nodes %>% filter(axis == "Virus Family") %>% select(name, target = id),
    by = c("Virus" = "name")
  ) %>%
  mutate(group = as.character(Host_Phylum)) %>%
  select(source, target, value, group)

links <- bind_rows(links1, links2, links3)

# -----------------------------
# 11. Convert to data frames for networkD3
# -----------------------------
nodes_df <- as.data.frame(nodes)
links_df <- as.data.frame(links)

# D3 color scale
colourScale <- sprintf(
  'd3.scaleOrdinal().range(["%s"])',
  paste(link_colors, collapse = '","')
)

# -----------------------------
# 12. Base Sankey plot
# -----------------------------
sankey_base <- sankeyNetwork(
  Links = links_df,
  Nodes = nodes_df,
  Source = "source",
  Target = "target",
  Value = "value",
  NodeID = "name",
  units = "T",
  fontSize = 15,
  fontFamily = "Arial",
  nodeWidth = 25,
  height = 600,
  width = 800,
  iterations = 64,
  nodePadding = 15,
  colourScale = colourScale
)

sankey_base

# -----------------------------
# 13. Add gradient links and styling
# -----------------------------
p_4b <- htmlwidgets::onRender(
  sankey_base,
  '
  function(el, x) {
    var svg = d3.select(el).select("svg");

    function createValidID(name) {
      if (!name) return "unknown";
      return name.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9-]/g, "_");
    }

    var nodesToRemove = [];
    svg.selectAll(".node").each(function(d) {
      if (!d.name || d.name === "NA") nodesToRemove.push(d.name);
    });

    nodesToRemove.forEach(function(nodeName) {
      svg.selectAll(".link").filter(function(link) {
        return link.target.name === nodeName;
      }).remove();

      svg.selectAll(".node").filter(function(node) {
        return node.name === nodeName;
      }).remove();
    });

    svg.selectAll(".link").each(function(d) {
      var gradientID = "gradient-" + createValidID(d.source.name) + "-" + createValidID(d.target.name);

      var gradient = svg.append("defs")
        .append("linearGradient")
        .attr("id", gradientID)
        .attr("gradientUnits", "userSpaceOnUse")
        .attr("x1", d.source.x + d.source.dx / 2)
        .attr("y1", d.source.y + d.source.dy / 2)
        .attr("x2", d.target.x + d.target.dx / 2)
        .attr("y2", d.target.y + d.target.dy / 2);

      var sourceColor = d3.select(el)
        .selectAll(".node")
        .filter(function(node) { return node.name === d.source.name; })
        .select("rect")
        .style("fill");

      var targetColor = d3.select(el)
        .selectAll(".node")
        .filter(function(node) { return node.name === d.target.name; })
        .select("rect")
        .style("fill");

      gradient.append("stop")
        .attr("offset", "0%")
        .attr("stop-color", sourceColor);

      gradient.append("stop")
        .attr("offset", "100%")
        .attr("stop-color", targetColor);

      d3.select(this).style("stroke", "url(#" + gradientID + ")");
    });

    // Make nodes semi-transparent
    svg.selectAll(".node rect")
      .style("fill-opacity", 0.5);

    // Make links more transparent
    svg.selectAll(".link")
      .style("stroke-opacity", 0.20);

    // Add black borders to nodes
    svg.selectAll(".node rect")
      .style("stroke", "black")
      .style("stroke-width", "1.5px");
  }
  '
)

p_4b

# -----------------------------
# 14. Export outputs
# -----------------------------
saveWidget(p_4b, file = "sankey_diagram3.html", selfcontained = TRUE)
webshot("sankey_diagram3.html", "sankey3.pdf")

```

## Fig. 4c
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Relative abundance area plots for host phyla and linked-vOTU phyla
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(tidyverse)   # ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, etc.
library(readr)       # fast input/output if needed

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
host_data <- readRDS("df_4c.rds")

df_host <- host_data$df_host
df_linked_vOTU <- host_data$df_linked_vOTU

# -----------------------------
# 3. Define sample order
# -----------------------------
# The first column is assumed to be "Phylum";
# all remaining columns are sample names
sample_order <- colnames(df_host)[-1]

# -----------------------------
# 4. Define phylum colors
# -----------------------------
phylum_colors <- c(
  "Others"              = "#CCCCCC",
  "Nanoarchaeota"       = "#D1BEDC",
  "Desulfobacterota_D"  = "#652F8D",
  "Gemmatimonadota"     = "#F0CFE3",
  "Nitrospinota"        = "#D090BD",
  "Desulfobacterota_B"  = "#E64392",
  "Chloroflexota"       = "#F6D467",
  "KSB1"                = "#F6FF55",
  "Bacteroidota"        = "#BA8D17",
  "Nitrospirota"        = "#C2DBB1",
  "JADFOP01"            = "#6EAA45",
  "Acidobacteriota"     = "#547F35",
  "Planctomycetota"     = "#DAE3F2",
  "Methylomirabilota"   = "#B3C4E5",
  "Pseudomonadota"      = "#8EA7D2",
  "Thermoproteota"      = "#2C5292"
)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Helper function:
#    convert wide abundance table to long format
#    and calculate relative abundance within each sample
# -----------------------------
prepare_relative_abundance <- function(df, sample_levels, color_levels) {
  df_long <- df %>%
    pivot_longer(
      -Phylum,
      names_to = "Sample",
      values_to = "Abundance"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Sample = factor(Sample, levels = sample_levels)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Sample) %>%
    mutate(
      Relative = Abundance / sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

  df_long$Phylum <- factor(df_long$Phylum, levels = color_levels)

  df_long
}

# -----------------------------
# 6. Prepare long-format data
# -----------------------------
df_long_host <- prepare_relative_abundance(
  df = df_host,
  sample_levels = sample_order,
  color_levels = names(phylum_colors)
)

df_long_linked_vOTU <- prepare_relative_abundance(
  df = df_linked_vOTU,
  sample_levels = sample_order,
  color_levels = names(phylum_colors)
)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Plot: host composition
# -----------------------------
p_4c_host <- ggplot(
  df_long_host,
  aes(x = Sample, y = Relative, fill = Phylum, group = Phylum)
) +
  geom_area(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = phylum_colors,
    drop = FALSE,
    breaks = names(phylum_colors)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Host proportion (%)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

p_4c_host

# -----------------------------
# 8. Plot: linked-vOTU composition
# -----------------------------
p_4c_virus <- ggplot(
  df_long_linked_vOTU,
  aes(x = Sample, y = Relative, fill = Phylum, group = Phylum)
) +
  geom_area(position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = phylum_colors,
    drop = FALSE,
    breaks = names(phylum_colors)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Viral proportion (%)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

p_4c_virus


```


## Fig. 5

## Fig. 5a
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Heatmap of AMG-associated vOTU profiles
# - Input matrix and annotation tables are loaded from an RDS file
# - Row and column clustering are disabled to preserve the input order
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
amg_heatmap_data <- readRDS("df_5a.rds")

heatmap_matrix <- amg_heatmap_data$df_5a
col_annotation <- amg_heatmap_data$datacol
row_annotation <- amg_heatmap_data$datarow

# -----------------------------
# 3. Define color palette
# -----------------------------
blues9 <- brewer.pal(9, "Blues")

# -----------------------------
# 4. Draw heatmap
#    Clustering is disabled so that the plot follows
#    the order already defined in the matrix and annotation tables
# -----------------------------
p_5a <- pheatmap(
  heatmap_matrix,
  annotation_col = col_annotation,
  annotation_row = row_annotation,
  clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
  color = blues9,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE
)


```


## Fig. 6

## Fig. 6a
```{r cars}
# =========================================================
# Gene map of viral genomes carrying HO-related regions
# =========================================================

# -----------------------------
# 1. Load packages
# -----------------------------
library(gggenes)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Load input data
# -----------------------------
# Required columns in df_6a:
# - start: gene start position
# - end: gene end position
# - viruses: viral genome / contig identifier
# - gene: gene category used for fill colors
# - strand: gene direction ("forward" or other)
gene_map_df <- readRDS("df_6a.rds")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Define color palette
# -----------------------------
gene_colors <- c(
  "AMG"        = "#e35025",
  "Viral-like" = "#edd365",
  "Others"     = "#bebdbd",
  "Hallmark"   = "#add8e5"
)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Plot gene map
# -----------------------------
p_6a <- gene_map_df %>%
  ggplot(
    aes(
      xmin = start,
      xmax = end,
      y = viruses,
      fill = gene,
      forward = strand == "forward"
    )
  ) +
  geom_gene_arrow(
    arrowhead_height = unit(6, "mm"),
    arrowhead_width = unit(1.5, "mm"),
    arrow_body_height = unit(4, "mm"),
    colour = NA,
    size = 0
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ viruses, scales = "free", ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = gene_colors) +
  theme_genes() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

p_6a
```


## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
